author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXI. 12, 13.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus went into the temple,and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the money-changers and the seats of them that sold doves, and saith unto them, It is written, my house shall be called a house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This John likewise saith, but he in the beginning of his Gospel, this at the end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is probable this was done twice, and at different seasons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And it is evident both from the times, and from their reply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there He came at the very passover, but here much before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And there the Jews say, “What sign showest thou us?”but here they hold their peace, although reproved, because He was now marvelled at amongst all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this is a heavier charge against the Jews, that when He had done this not once only, but a second time, they continued in their trafficking, and said that He was an adversary of God, when they ought even from hence to have learnt His honor for His Father and His own might.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed He also wrought miracles, and they saw His words agreeing with His works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even so were they persuaded, but “were sore displeased,” and this while they heard the prophet crying aloud, and the children in a manner beyond their age proclaiming Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He Himself sets up Isaiah against them as an accuser, saying, “My house shall be called a house of prayer.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not in this way only doth He show His authority, but also by His healing divers infirmities.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the blind and the lame came unto Him, and He healed them,”and His power and authority He indicates.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they not even so would be persuaded, but together with the rest of the miracles hearing even the children proclaiming, were ready to choke, and say, “Hearest thou not what these say?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet it was Christ’s part to have said this to them, “Hear ye not what these say?” for the children were singing to Him as to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since they were speaking against things manifest, He applies His correction more in the way of reproof, saying, “Have ye never read, Out of the mouths of babes and sucklings Thou hast perfected praise?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And well did He say, “Out of the mouth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what was said was not of their understanding, but of His power giving articulation to their tongue yet immature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this was also a type of the Gentiles lisping, and sounding forth at once great things with understanding and faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for the apostles also there was from hence no small consolation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they might not be perplexed, how being unlearned they should be able to publish the gospel, the children anticipate them, and remove all their anxiety, teaching them, that He would grant them utterance, who made even these to sing praises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not so only, but the miracle showed that He is Creator even of nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The children then, although of age immature, uttered things that had a clear meaning, and were in accordance with those above, but the men things teeming with frenzy and madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the nature of wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as there were many things to provoke them, from the multitude, from the casting out of the sellers, from the miracles, from the children, He again leaves them, giving room to the swelling passion, and not willing to begin His teaching, lest boiling with envy they should be the more displeased at His sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now in the morning as He returned into the city, He was an hungered.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How is He an hungered in the morning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He permits the flesh, then it shows its feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when He saw a fig tree in the way, He came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Another evangelist saith, “The time of figs was not yet;”but if it was not time, how doth the other evangelist say, “He came, if haply He might find fruit thereon.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is manifest that this belongs to the suspicion of His disciples, who were yet in a somewhat imperfect state.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed the evangelists in many places record the suspicions of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Like as this then was their suspicion, so also was it too to suppose it was cursed for this cause, because of having no fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then was it cursed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the disciples’ sakes, that they might have confidence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because everywhere He conferred benefits, but punished no man; and it was needful that He should afford them a demonstrative proof of His power to take vengeance also, that both the disciples might learn, and the Jews, that being able to blast them that crucify Him, of His own will He submits, and does not blast them; and it was not His will to show forth this upon men; upon the plant did He furnish the proof of His might in taking vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when unto places, or unto plants, or unto brutes, any such thing as this is done, be not curious, neither say, how was the fig-tree justly dried up, if it was not the time of figs; for this it is the utmost trifling to say; but behold the miracle, and admire and glorify the worker thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since in the case also of the swine that were drowned, many have said this, working out the argument of justice; but neither there should one give heed, for these again are brutes, even as that was a plant without life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then was the act invested with such an appearance, and with this plea for a curse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As I said, this was the disciple’s suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if it was not yet time, vainly do some say the law is here meant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the fruit of this was faith, and then was the time of this fruit, and it had indeed borne it; “For alreadyare the fields white to harvest,” saith He; and, “I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not any therefore of these things doth He here intimate, but it is what I said, He displays His power to punish, and this is shown by saying, “The time was not yet,” making it clear that of this special purpose He went, and not for hunger, but for His disciples’ sake, who indeed marvelled exceedingly, although many miracles had been done greater; but, as I said, this was strange, for now first He showed forth His power to take vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore not in any other, but in the moistest of all planted things did He work the miracle, so that hence also the miracle appeared greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that thou mightest learn, that for their sakes this was done, that He might train them to feel confidence, hear what He saith afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye also shall do greater things, if ye are willing to believe and to be confident in prayer.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that all is done for their sake, so that they might not be afraid and tremble at plots against them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith this a second time also, to make them cleave to prayer and faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For not this only shall ye do, but also shall remove mountains; and many more things shall ye do, being confident in faith and prayer.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the boastful and arrogant Jews, wishing to interrupt His teaching, came unto Him, and asked, “By what authority doest thou these things?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since they could not object against the miracles, they bring forward against Him the correction of the traffickers in the temple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this in John also they appear to ask, although not in these words, but with the same intent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there too they say, “What sign showest thou unto us? seeing that thou doest these things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But there He answers them, saying, “Destroy this temple, and I in three days will raise it up,”whereas here He drives them into a difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is manifest, that then indeed was the beginning and prelude of the miracles, but here the end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what they say is this: Hast thou received the teacher’s chair?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou been ordained a priest, that thou didst display such authority? it is said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet He had done nothing implying arrogance, but had been careful for the good order of the temple, yet nevertheless having nothing to say, they object against this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed when He cast them out, they did not dare to say anything, because of the miracles, but when He showed Himself, then they find fault with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He doth not answer them directly, to show that, if they had been willing to see His authority, they could; but He asks them again, saying, “The baptism of John, whence is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From heaven, or of men?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what sort of inference is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The greatest surely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they had said, from heaven, He would have said unto them, why then did ye not believe him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they had believed, they would not have asked these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of Him John had said, “I am not worthy to loose the latchet of His shoe;” and, “Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sins of the world;” and, “This is the Son of God;”and, “He that cometh from above is above all;”and, “His fan is in His hand, and He will thoroughly purge His floor.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if they had believed him, there was nothing to hinder them from knowing by what authority Christ doeth these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, because they, dealing craftily, said, “We know not,” He said not, neither know I, but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Neither tell I you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if indeed they had been ignorant it would have been requisite for them to be instructed; but since they were dealing craftily with good reason He answers them nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how was it they did not say that the baptism was of men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “They feared the people”it is said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou a perverse heart?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In every case they despise God and do all things for the sake of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this man too they feared for their sakes not reverencing the saintbut on account of men,and they were not willing to believe in Christ, because of men, and all their evils were engendered to them from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, He saith, “What think ye?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A man had two sons; and he saith to the first, go, work to-day in the vineyard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But he answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he came to the second, and said likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he answered and said, I go sir: and went not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whether then of them twain did the will of his father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They say, the first.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again He convicts them by a parable, intimating both their unreasonable obstinacy, and the submissiveness of those who were utterly condemned by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these two children declare what came to pass with respect to both the Gentiles and the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the former not having undertaken to obey, neither having become hearers of the law, showed forth their obedience in their works; and the latter having said, “All that the Lord shall speak, we will do, and will hearken,”in their works were disobedient.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for this reason, let me add, that they might not think the law would benefit them, He shows that this self-same thing condemns them, like as Paul also saith, “Not the hearers of the law are just before God, but the doers of the law shall be justified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this intent, that He might make them even self-condemned, He causes the judgment to be delivered by themselves, like as He does also in the ensuing parable of the vineyard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that this might be done, He makes trial of the accusation in the person of an other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since they were not willing to confess directly, He by a parable drives them on to what He desired.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when, not understanding His sayings, they had delivered the judgment, He unfolds His concealed meaning after this, and saith, “Publicans and harlots go into the kingdom of Heaven before you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not; but the publicansbelieved him; and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterwards, that ye might believe him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He had said simply, harlots go before you, the word would have seemed to them to be offensive; but now, being uttered after their own judgment it appears to be not too hard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He adds also the accusation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “John came,” He saith, “unto you,” not unto them, and not this only, but; also “in the way of righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For neither with this can ye find fault, that he was some careless one, and of no profit; but both his life was irreprehensible, and his care for you great, and ye gave no heed to him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And with this there is another charge also, that publicans gave heed; and with this, again another, that “not even after them did ye.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For ye should have done so even before them, but not to do it even after them was to be deprived of all excuse;” and unspeakable was both the praise of the one, and the charge against the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “To you he came, and ye accepted him not; he came not to them, and they receive him, and not even them did ye take for instructors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See by how many things is shown the commendation of those, and the charge against these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To you he came, not to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye believed not, this offended not them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They believed, this profited not you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the word, “go before you,” is not as though these were following, but as having a hope, if they were willing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing, so much as jealousy, rouses the grosser sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He is ever saying, “The first shall be last, and the last first.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He brought in both harlots and publicans, that they might provoke them to jealousy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these two indeed are chief sins, engendered of violent lust, the one of sexual desire, the other of the desire of money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He indicates that this especially was hearing the law of God, to believe John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was not of grace only, that harlots entered in, but also of righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not, as continuing harlots, did they enter in, but having obeyed and believed, and having been purified and converted, so did they enter in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He rendered His discourse less offensive, and more penetrating, by the parable, by His bringing in the harlots?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did He say at once, wherefore believed ye not John? but what was much more pricking, when, He had put forward the publicans and the harlots, then He added this, by the order of their actions convicting their unpardonable conduct, and showing that for fear of men they do all things, and for vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they did not confess Christ for fear, lest they should be put out of the synagogue; and again, of John they dared not speak evil, and not even this from reverence, but for fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All which things He convicted by His sayings, and with more severity afterwards did He go on to inflict the blow, saying, “But ye, when ye knew it, repented not afterwards, that ye might believe him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For an evil thing it is not at the first to choose the good, but it is a heavier charge not even to be brought round.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this above all maketh many wicked, which I see to be the case with some now from extreme insensibility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let no one be like this; but though he be sunk down to the extremity of wickedness, let him not despair of the change for the better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is an easy thing to rise up out of the very abysses of wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heard ye not how that harlot, that went beyond all in lasciviousness, outshone all in godly reverence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not the harlot in the gospels do I mean, but the one in our generation, who came from Phœnice, that most lawless city.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For she was once a harlot among us, having the first honors on the stage, and great was her name everywhere, not in our city only, but even as far as the Cilicians and Cappadocians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And many estates did she ruin, and many orphans did she overthrow; and many accused her of sorcery also, as weaving such toils not by her beauty of person only, but also by her drugs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This harlot once won even the brother of the empress, for mighty indeed was her tyranny.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But all at once, I know not how, or rather I do know well, for it was being so minded, and converting, and bringing down upon herself God’s grace, she despised all those things, and having cast away the arts of the devils, mounted up to heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed nothing was more vile than she was, when she was on the stage; nevertheless, afterwards she outwent many in exceeding continence, and having clad herself with sackcloth, all her time she thus disciplined herself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On the account of this woman both the governor was stirred up, and soldiers armed, yet they had not strength to carry her off to the stage, nor to lead her away from the virgins that had received her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This woman having been counted worthy of the unutterable mysteries, and having exhibited a diligence proportionate to the grace (given her) so ended her life, having washed off all through grace, and after her baptism having shown forth much self-restraint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not even a mere sight of herself did she allow to those who were once her lovers, when they had come for this, having shut herself up, and having passed many years, as it were, in a prison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus “shall the last be first, and the first last;” thus do we in every case need a fervent soul, and there is nothing to hinder one from becoming great and admirable:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no man then of them that live in vice despair; let no man who lives in virtue slumber.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let neither this last be confident, for often the harlot will pass him by; nor let the other despair, for it is possible for him to pass by even the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear what God saith unto Jerusalem, “I said, after she had committed all these whoredoms, Turn thou unto me, and she returned not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When we have come back unto the earnest love of God, He remembers not the former things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God is not as man, for He reproaches us not with the past, neither doth He say, Why wast thou absent so long a time? when we repent; only let us approach Him as we ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us cleave to Him earnestly, and rivet our hearts to His fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such things have been done not under the new covenant only, but even under the old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what was worse than Manasseh? but he was able to appease God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What more blessed than Solomon? but when he slumbered, he fell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather I can show even both things to have taken place in one, in the father of this man, for he the same person became at different times both good and bad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What more blessed than Judas? but he became a traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What more wretched than Matthew? but he became an evangelist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What worse than Paul? but he became an apostle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What more to be envied than Simon? but he became even himself the most wretched of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How many other such changes wouldest thou see, both to have taken place of old, and now taking place every day?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason then I say, Neither let him on the stave despair, nor let him in the church be confident.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to this last it is said, “Let him that thinketh he standeth, take heed lest he fall;”and to the other, “Shall not he that falleth arise?”and, “Lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, to these He saith, “Watch;” but to those, “Awake, thou that sleepest and arise from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these need to preserve what they have, and those to become what they are not; these to preserve their health, those to be delivered from their infirmity, for they are sick; but many even of the sick become healthy, and of the healthy many by remissness grow infirm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To the one then He saith, “Behold, thou art made whole, sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee;”but to these, “Wilt thou be made whole?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For a dreadful, dreadful palsy is sin, or rather it is not palsy only, but also somewhat else more grievous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such a one is not only in inactivity as to good works, but also in the active doing of evil works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nevertheless, though thou be so disposed, and be willing to rouse thyself a little, all the terrors are at an end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though thou hast been so “thirty and eight years,” and art earnest to become whole, there is no one to hinder thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Christ is present now also, and saith, “Take up thy bed,” only be willing to rouse thyself, despair not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou no man? but thou hast God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou no one to put thee into the pool? but thou hast Him who suffers thee not to need the pool.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou had no one to cast thee in there? but thou hast Him that commands thee to take up thy bed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou mayest not say, “While I am coming, another steppeth down before me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if it be thy will to go down into the fountain, there is none to hinder thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Grace is not consumed, is not spent, it is a kind of fountain springing up constantly; by His fullness are we all healed both soul and body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us come unto it then even now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Rahab also was a harlot, yet was she saved; and the thief was a murderer, yet he became a citizen of paradise; and while Judas being with his Master perished, the thief being on a cross became a disciple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such are the wonderful works of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus the magi approved themselves, thus the publican became an evangelist, thus the blasphemer an apostle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Look at these things, and never despair, but be ever confident, and rouse thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lay hold only on the way that leads thither, and thou wilt advance quickly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shut not up the doors, close not up the entrance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Short is the present life, small the labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But though it were great, not even so ought one to decline it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou toil not at this most glorious toil that is spent upon repentance and virtue, in the world thou wilt assuredly toil and weary thyself in other ways.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if both in the one and the other there be labor, why do we not choose that which hath its fruit abundant, and its recompense greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet neither is this labor and that the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in worldly pursuits are continual perils, and losses one upon another, and the hope uncertain; great is the servility, and the expenditure alike of wealth, and of bodies, and of souls; and then the return of the fruits is far below our expectation, if perchance it should grow up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither doth toil upon worldly matters everywhere bear fruit; nay but even, when it hath not failed, but has brought forth its produce even abundantly, short is the time wherein it continues.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when thou art grown old, and hast no longer after that the feeling of enjoyment in perfection, then and not till then doth the labor bear thee its recompense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas the labor was with the body in its vigor, the fruit and the enjoyment is with one grown old and languid, when time has dulled even the feeling, although if it had not dulled it, the expectation of the end suffers us not to find pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But in the other case not so, but the labor is in corruption and a dying body, but the crown in one incorruptible, and immortal, and having no end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the labor is both first and short-lived; but the reward both subsequent and endless, that with security thou mayest take thy rest after that, looking for nothing unpleasant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither mayest thou fear change any more or loss as here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sort of good things, then, are these, which are both insecure, and short-lived, and earthly, and vanishing before they have appeared, and acquired with many toils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what good things are equal to those, that are immovable, that grow not old, that have no toil, that even at the time of the conflicts bring thee crowns?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that despises money even here already receives his reward, being freed from anxiety, from rivalry, from false accusation, from plotting from envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that is temperate, and lives orderly, even before his departure, is crowned and lives in pleasure, being delivered from unseemliness, ridicule, dangers of accusation,and the other things that are to be feared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All the remaining parts of virtue likewise make us a return here already.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order therefore that we may attain unto both the present and the future blessings, let us flee from vice and choose virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus shall we both enjoy delight, and obtain the crowns to come, unto which God grant we may all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
